results.reason: A continuous dense tree-line rings the whole settlement; it is a clearly bounded place, not open field.
results.title: Bounded perimeter

results.reason: Stone paths branch organically: a main spine splits into irregular offshoots that wrap around the buildings.
results.title: Organic branching paths

results.reason: Paths branch well and reach the building fronts, but the central paved area is a large open expanse so compression/release is softer than Map017.
results.title: Path rhythm and door alignment

results.reason: Buildings use genuinely different materials (pink, yellow-stone, brown, tan) with some sharing walls into a cluster.
results.title: Varied building massing

results.reason: Several distinct build materials give a strong varied settlement palette rather than a re-tinted ground.
results.title: Distinct material identity

results.reason: A fenced farm plot with crops, wells, lampposts, barrels and NPCs are distributed through the interior and clustered in pockets.
results.title: Decoration density

results.reason: The central well-and-lamppost plaza anchors the first read.
results.title: Dominant landmark

results.reason: It reads immediately as an enclosed village with a civic centre.
results.title: Readable first view

results.reason: Searchable barrels and crates are grouped beside buildings rather than isolated.
results.title: Dressed reward clusters

results.reason: A well provides a landmark but there is no open water feature, so this reads softer than Map017's river.
results.title: Water or organic plaza

results.reason: Grass, stone path, timber and stone-wall vocabulary is reused coherently across the map.
results.title: Limited coherent vocabulary

results.reason: The walled village with the central plaza and farm is easy to recall and state.
results.title: Memorable identity
